params.name: Cloth Monkey
params.description: Harry Harlow's experiment with cloth monkeys revealed the significance of tactile comfort in the mother-infant relationship among primates. Newborn monkeys, separated from their biological mothers, were given a choice between a wire surrogate mother that provided milk and a cloth surrogate mother that offered no sustenance. The results showed that the infant monkeys spent most of their time clinging to the cloth surrogate, only visiting the wire surrogate to feed when hungry. In moments of distr …
